Dear Senator Garamendi: 
July 6, 1977 
7 - cc; --
71 
Oll May 6, 1977, you sent .a letter to Dawn Wiser 
of the Commission staff in which you questioned whether you 
were required to disclose the assets of a revocable trust 
which names your wife as _a beneficiary. 
By letter dated 
May 20, 1977, I indicated that the staff had interpreted the 
Act to require such disclosure. 
At a recent meeting, the ~ommission directed the 
staff to reconsider its advice to:'you and to reassess gen-
erally the treatment of trusts in the Political Reform Act 
with an eye to possible revision by regulation or legisla-
tive amendment. 
The staff is presently engaged in such a 
reexamination. 
In the meantime, the commission has asked 
me to advise you that yonnead not disclose the interest in 
question until we have cQlDpleted our review. 
We would welcome any Ad4~tional comments you may 
kAve on this matter.

Dawn Wiser 
Sincerely, 
~ichael J. Baker

sections.conclusion: The Commission has asked me to advise you that you need not disclose the interest in question until we have completed our review. The staff is presently engaged in a reexamination of the treatment of trusts in the Political Reform Act with an eye to possible revision by regulation or legislative amendment.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a state senator disclose assets held in a revocable trust in which his spouse is named as a beneficiary under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Senator Garamendi need not disclose the revocable trust interest at this time while the FPPC staff reconsiders its prior interpretation requiring such disclosure and reassesses the general treatment of trusts under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request from Senator Garamendi (May 6, 1977) and the FPPC response letter (July 6, 1977) from Michael J. Baker, Chief of Legal Division. The FPPC is reconsidering its initial staff position and suspending the disclosure requirement pending further review. OCR quality is generally good with minor degradation in some sections.

embedding.summary: The FPPC reverses its prior May 20, 1977 advice to Senator Garamendi requiring disclosure of assets in a revocable trust naming his wife as beneficiary, directing that no disclosure is required pending the Commission's ongoing review of trust treatment under the Political Reform Act.
